title: Corymbis thouarsii

This classification places it within the order Malpighiales, which is a large group of flowering plants that are commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. Corymbis thouarsii is likely a relatively rare or lesser-known species within this family. Further research would be needed to determine the specific classification and taxonomic relationships of Corymbis thouarsii.
uses: Corymbis thouarsii has been traditionally used in African medicine, particularly in Madagascar. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems.
